title: Internal Component Error
shortDescription: A critical internal hardware error has occurred in the printer.
fullDescription: Error 0x97 on Epson printers indicates a fatal internal hardware error, often related to the mainboard or printhead assembly. The printer will not function until the error is cleared or the component is replaced.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 5 minutes, then plug back in.

fixSteps.title: Remove Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the printer and remove any paper jams or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect USB
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug all USB cables, power cycle, then reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Clean Printhead Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the printhead and gently clean the electrical contacts with a lint-free cloth dampened with distilled water.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling and cleaning contacts does not resolve the error.

faq.a: Sometimes power cycling works. If not, the printhead or mainboard may need replacement.
If the printer cost under $200, replacement is often more economical than board repair.
Power surges, age, and printhead wear are common causes.

title: Scanner Unit Error
shortDescription: The scanner unit has encountered a hardware error.
fullDescription: Error 0x9A indicates the scanner mechanism or sensor has failed. This affects scanning, copying, and any function that requires the scanner unit.

fixSteps.title: Clean Glass
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the scanner glass and underside of the lid with glass cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Check for Objects
fixSteps.instruction: Open the scanner and check for paper clips, staples, or paper scraps.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check Cable
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable, open the unit and verify the scanner flat cable is properly seated.

whenToCallPro: If the scanner motor makes grinding noises or the cable appears damaged.

faq.q: Can I still print with this error?
faq.a: On some models, printing may still work even when scanning is disabled.
Yes, but it requires disassembly — often not cost-effective on budget models.
Age, mechanical wear, and foreign objects are the main causes.

title: Paper Feed Error
shortDescription: The paper feed mechanism cannot pick up or advance paper.
fullDescription: Error 0xEA indicates the paper feed rollers are unable to grip and feed paper. This is typically caused by worn or dirty rollers, improper paper loading, or a paper jam.

fixSteps.title: Reload Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all paper, fan the stack, and reload it properly in the tray.

fixSteps.title: Clean Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Dampen a lint-free cloth with water and gently wipe the paper feed rollers.

fixSteps.title: Run Cleaning Utility
fixSteps.instruction: Access the printer menu and run the paper feed roller cleaning utility if available.

fixSteps.title: Check for Debris
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the paper path for torn paper, labels, or debris.

whenToCallPro: If rollers are visibly worn and cleaning does not help.

faq.a: Roller replacement kits are available for some models — check Epson support.

faq.q: What type of paper works best?
faq.a: Use standard 20lb copy paper. Glossy or thick paper can cause feed issues.

faq.q: How often should I clean rollers?
faq.a: Every few months or when you notice feed problems.

title: General Printer Error
shortDescription: A general hardware error has occurred.
fullDescription: Error E-01 is a generic catch-all error on Epson printers indicating an internal hardware fault. It often requires a power cycle or service.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 60 seconds, and turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and check for paper jams, debris, or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Update Driver
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest driver from Epson's support website.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset through the printer's settings menu.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Epson error E-01 mean?
faq.a: A general hardware error has occurred.
This is a frequently reported error on Epson printers.
If the printer is under 3 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older printers may be more economical to replace.

title: Paper Jam Detected
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside the printer.
fullDescription: Error E-02 indicates paper is stuck in the paper path. The printer will not operate until the jam is completely cleared.

causes.cause: Printer not registered with Epson Connect

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 60 seconds, and turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and check for paper jams, debris, or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Update Driver
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest driver from Epson's support website.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset through the printer's settings menu.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Epson error E-600 mean?
faq.a: The printer cannot connect to Epson Connect cloud services.
This is a frequently reported error on Epson printers.
If the printer is under 3 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older printers may be more economical to replace.

title: Fax Communication Error
shortDescription: The fax function failed to send or receive.
fullDescription: Error E-700 indicates a failure in the fax communication process — the call could not be completed or the handshake with the remote fax failed.

causes.cause: Remote fax machine busy or offline
